title: Use the event_loop fixture
body:
Refs:
- https://github.com/simonw/datasette/issues/2461#issuecomment-2634920351

<!-- readthedocs-preview datasette start -->
----
📚 Documentation preview 📚: https://datasette--2463.org.readthedocs.build/en/2463/

<!-- readthedocs-preview datasette end -->

step_plan.summary: Replace two pytest-asyncio async fixtures (ds_client and datasette) with synchronous fixtures that accept pytest's event_loop and run async operations using event_loop.run_until_complete, removing the pytest_asyncio decorator and its import where unused.

steps.operation: Convert async ds_client fixture to use pytest's event_loop fixture
steps.reason: Remove reliance on pytest-asyncio's @pytest_asyncio.fixture by making the fixture synchronous and explicitly using pytest's event_loop fixture. Inside the new def ds_client(event_loop): run previously awaited coroutines (for example db.execute_write_fn(prepare) and ds.invoke_startup()) via event_loop.run_until_complete(...). Replace the @pytest_asyncio.fixture decorator with @pytest.fixture and remove the pytest_asyncio import if it becomes unused.
steps.side_effects: May change lifecycle interactions with the event loop: ds_client currently caches a Datasette instance in a module-global _ds_client and may therefore be reused across tests that get different event_loop instances. Reusing the same Datasette across different event loops could surface lifecycle or runtime errors if underlying objects hold loop references. Running the test suite may reveal loop-lifetime issues; if so, adjust the caching scope or avoid cross-loop reuse. No public API changes; only tests are affected. Dependent test fixtures and async tests that rely on ds_client should continue to work, but ensure they run under the same event loop semantics. Remove of pytest_asyncio import may affect nothing else if it was only used for the decorator.

steps.operation: Convert async datasette fixture to use pytest's event_loop fixture
steps.reason: Replace @pytest_asyncio.fixture async def datasette(ds_client): with @pytest.fixture def datasette(ds_client, event_loop): and execute the awaited startup coroutine with event_loop.run_until_complete(ds_client.ds.invoke_startup()). This makes event loop usage explicit and avoids pytest-asyncio's async fixture decorator.
steps.side_effects: Since datasette calls ds_client.ds.invoke_startup(), moving it to run on an explicitly provided event_loop ensures the startup runs on the same loop as other test coroutines. If ds_client is cached across tests with different loops (see previous step), calling invoke_startup() on a Datasette instance created for another loop may cause errors. Tests relying on this fixture may need to be re-run to detect such issues. No changes to production code or external APIs. If pytest_asyncio was only imported for the decorator, its removal here is safe. Ensure async tests marked with @pytest.mark.asyncio remain unchanged.
